speaker: MACBETH
text: You know your own degrees; sit down. At first

speaker: MACBETH
text: And last, the hearty welcome.

speaker: LORDS
text: Thanks to your Majesty.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ourself will mingle with society

speaker: MACBETH
text: And play the humble host.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Our hostess keeps her state, but in best time

speaker: MACBETH
text: We will require her welcome.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Pronounce it for me, sir, to all our friends,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: For my heart speaks they are welcome.

speaker: MACBETH
text: See, they encounter thee with their hearts’ thanks.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Both sides are even. Here I’ll sit i’ th’ midst.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Be large in mirth. Anon we’ll drink a measure

speaker: MACBETH
text:
The table round.
                 
                He
                 
                approaches
                 
                the
                 
                Murderer
                .
               There’s blood upon thy face.

speaker: MURDERER
text: ’Tis Banquo’s then.

speaker: MACBETH
text: ’Tis better thee without than he within.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Is he dispatched?

speaker: MURDERER
text: My lord, his throat is cut. That I did for him.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou art the best o’ th’ cutthroats,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Yet he’s good that did the like for Fleance.

speaker: MACBETH
text: If thou didst it, thou art the nonpareil.

speaker: MURDERER
text: Most royal sir, Fleance is ’scaped.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Then comes my fit again. I had else been perfect,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Whole as the marble, founded as the rock,

speaker: MACBETH
text: As broad and general as the casing air.

speaker: MACBETH
text: But now I am cabined, cribbed, confined, bound in

speaker: MACBETH
text: To saucy doubts and fears.—But Banquo’s safe?

speaker: MURDERER
text: Ay, my good lord. Safe in a ditch he bides,

speaker: MURDERER
text: With twenty trenchèd gashes on his head,

speaker: MURDERER
text: The least a death to nature.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thanks for that.

speaker: MACBETH
text: There the grown serpent lies. The worm that’s fled

speaker: MACBETH
text: Hath nature that in time will venom breed,

speaker: MACBETH
text: No teeth for th’ present. Get thee gone. Tomorrow

speaker: MACBETH
text: We’ll hear ourselves again.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: My royal lord,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: You do not give the cheer. The feast is sold

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That is not often vouched, while ’tis a-making,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: ’Tis given with welcome. To feed were best at home;

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: From thence, the sauce to meat is ceremony;

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Meeting were bare without it.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Sweet remembrancer!—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Now, good digestion wait on appetite

speaker: MACBETH
text: And health on both!

speaker: LENNOX
text: May ’t please your Highness sit.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Here had we now our country’s honor roofed,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Were the graced person of our Banquo present,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Who may I rather challenge for unkindness

speaker: MACBETH
text: Than pity for mischance.

speaker: ROSS
text: His absence, sir,

speaker: ROSS
text: Lays blame upon his promise. Please ’t your Highness

speaker: ROSS
text: To grace us with your royal company?

speaker: MACBETH
text: The table’s full.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Here is a place reserved, sir.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Where?

speaker: LENNOX
text: Here, my good lord. What is ’t that moves your Highness?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which of you have done this?

speaker: LORDS
text: What, my good lord?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou canst not say I did it. Never shake

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thy gory locks at me.

speaker: ROSS
text: Gentlemen, rise. His Highness is not well.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Sit, worthy friends. My lord is often thus

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And hath been from his youth. Pray you, keep seat.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: The fit is momentary; upon a thought

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: He will again be well. If much you note him

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: You shall offend him and extend his passion.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Feed and regard him not.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Are you a man?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ay, and a bold one, that dare look on that

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which might appall the devil.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: O, proper stuff!

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: This is the very painting of your fear.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: This is the air-drawn dagger which you said

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Led you to Duncan. O, these flaws and starts,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Impostors to true fear, would well become

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: A woman’s story at a winter’s fire,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Authorized by her grandam. Shame itself!

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Why do you make such faces? When all’s done,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: You look but on a stool.

speaker: MACBETH
text:
Prithee, see there. Behold, look!
                 
                To
                 
                the
                 
                Ghost
                .
               Lo, how say you?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Why, what care I? If thou canst nod, speak too.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: If charnel houses and our graves must send

speaker: MACBETH
text: Those that we bury back, our monuments

speaker: MACBETH
text: Shall be the maws of kites.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: What, quite unmanned in folly?

speaker: MACBETH
text: If I stand here, I saw him.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Fie, for shame!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Blood hath been shed ere now, i’ th’ olden time,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ere humane statute purged the gentle weal;

speaker: MACBETH
text: Ay, and since too, murders have been performed

speaker: MACBETH
text: Too terrible for the ear. The time has been

speaker: MACBETH
text: That, when the brains were out, the man would die,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And there an end. But now they rise again

speaker: MACBETH
text: With twenty mortal murders on their crowns

speaker: MACBETH
text: And push us from our stools. This is more strange

speaker: MACBETH
text: Than such a murder is.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: My worthy lord,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Your noble friends do lack you.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I do forget.—

speaker: MACBETH
text: Do not muse at me, my most worthy friends.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I have a strange infirmity, which is nothing

speaker: MACBETH
text: To those that know me. Come, love and health to all.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Then I’ll sit down.—Give me some wine. Fill full.

speaker: MACBETH
text: I drink to th’ general joy o’ th’ whole table

speaker: MACBETH
text: And to our dear friend Banquo, whom we miss.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Would he were here! To all, and him we thirst,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And all to all.

speaker: LORDS
text: Our duties, and the pledge.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Avaunt, and quit my sight! Let the earth hide thee.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thy bones are marrowless; thy blood is cold;

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou hast no speculation in those eyes

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which thou dost glare with.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Think of this, good peers,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: But as a thing of custom. ’Tis no other;

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Only it spoils the pleasure of the time.

speaker: MACBETH
text: What man dare, I dare.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Approach thou like the rugged Russian bear,

speaker: MACBETH
text: The armed rhinoceros, or th’ Hyrcan tiger;

speaker: MACBETH
text: Take any shape but that, and my firm nerves

speaker: MACBETH
text: Shall never tremble. Or be alive again

speaker: MACBETH
text: And dare me to the desert with thy sword.

speaker: MACBETH
text: If trembling I inhabit then, protest me

speaker: MACBETH
text: The baby of a girl. Hence, horrible shadow!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Unreal mock’ry, hence!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Why so, being gone,

speaker: MACBETH
text: I am a man again.—Pray you sit still.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: You have displaced the mirth, broke the good meeting

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: With most admired disorder.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Can such things be

speaker: MACBETH
text: And overcome us like a summer’s cloud,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Without our special wonder? You make me strange

speaker: MACBETH
text: Even to the disposition that I owe

speaker: MACBETH
text: When now I think you can behold such sights

speaker: MACBETH
text: And keep the natural ruby of your cheeks

speaker: MACBETH
text: When mine is blanched with fear.

speaker: ROSS
text: What sights, my lord?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: I pray you, speak not. He grows worse and worse.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Question enrages him. At once, good night.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Stand not upon the order of your going,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: But go at once.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Good night, and better health

speaker: LENNOX
text: Attend his Majesty.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: A kind good night to all.

speaker: MACBETH
text: It will have blood, they say; blood will have blood.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Stones have been known to move, and trees to speak.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Augurs and understood relations have

speaker: MACBETH
text: By maggot pies and choughs and rooks brought forth

speaker: MACBETH
text: The secret’st man of blood.—What is the night?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Almost at odds with morning, which is which.

speaker: MACBETH
text: How say’st thou that Macduff denies his person

speaker: MACBETH
text: At our great bidding?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Did you send to him, sir?

speaker: MACBETH
text: I hear it by the way; but I will send.

speaker: MACBETH
text: There’s not a one of them but in his house

speaker: MACBETH
text: I keep a servant fee’d. I will tomorrow

speaker: MACBETH
text: (And betimes I will) to the Weïrd Sisters.

speaker: MACBETH
text: More shall they speak, for now I am bent to know

speaker: MACBETH
text: By the worst means the worst. For mine own good,

speaker: MACBETH
text: All causes shall give way. I am in blood

speaker: MACBETH
text: Stepped in so far that, should I wade no more,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Returning were as tedious as go o’er.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Strange things I have in head that will to hand,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Which must be acted ere they may be scanned.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: You lack the season of all natures, sleep.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Come, we’ll to sleep. My strange and self-abuse

speaker: MACBETH
text: Is the initiate fear that wants hard use.

speaker: MACBETH
text: We are yet but young in deed.
